name: Prose: "A Visitor from Distant Lands"

sourceRefs.label: civilserviceaspirants.in - dedicated MCQ set (6 questions); samacheerkalvi.guru - full plot summary including the chilli/Columbus backstory

researchNotes: OCR index search for 'visitor from distant lands' returned zero hits — web research only. A two-part educational story: a frame narrative about a boy, Mani, refusing to eat potato curry because he believes potatoes are 'foreign', nested around the real historical story of how chillies travelled from South America to India via Christopher Columbus and the Portuguese.

reading.intro: A story about Mani, a village boy near Senji (Villupuram district), who refuses to eat his favourite potato curry after his teacher tells him potatoes are a 'foreign' vegetable — used as a frame to teach the real history of how New World foods like chillies and potatoes reached India via European exploration and trade.

qa.question: Where is the story set, and who are the two children in it?
qa.answer: In a village near Senji, in Villupuram district. The two children are Mani and his sister Selvi.

qa.question: What food did Mani refuse to eat, and why?
qa.answer: Potato curry (his usual favourite dish) — he refused because his TEACHER had told him potatoes are a foreign vegetable, and he wanted to eat only locally-grown food.

qa.question: Where did Mani's mother get the potatoes from?
What is the real historical story of the chilli, told within this lesson?
qa.answer: Chillies originally grew only in South America, unknown to the rest of the world. Christopher Columbus — while actually searching for a sea route to India to buy black pepper — instead reached South America and discovered the chilli there. Chillies were later brought to India by the Portuguese, first arriving in Goa, where local cooks began incorporating this new spice into their cuisine.

qa.question: What spice did Indian cooking commonly rely on BEFORE chillies arrived?

questions.prompt: Where is the story "A Visitor from Distant Lands" set?
questions.explanation: A village near Senji, Villupuram district.

questions.prompt: Who are the two children in the story?
